denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter concerns the prior authorization request for a levonorgestrel 52 mg intrauterine device placement submitted on your behalf. After review under the UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines, United has determined that this service is not authorized for coverage.

The requesting gynecologist is not a participating provider in your plan's network. Plan benefits for this procedure require that care be furnished by an in-network provider, or that a gap exception be approved in advance when no participating provider is reasonably available. Our records show that participating gynecologists in your service area were available and that no gap exception was requested before the procedure. On that basis the service is considered out-of-network care obtained without authorization.

Separately, the Optum clinical reviewer found that the submitted documentation does not support medical necessity under United's coverage criteria. Those criteria reflect the efficacy data established in the adolescent population, ages 12 to 17, and call for a documented trial of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs and combined oral contraceptives before an intrauterine device is considered for dysmenorrhea. The documentation does not demonstrate that this requirement was met.

Your provider's request was received at 2:14 PM on May 21, 2026, and our determination was completed at 2:17 PM the same day. The decision reflects review of the information available to United at that time.

You have the right to request a copy of your claim file and the specific clinical criteria applied to this determination at no charge. You may appeal this decision within 180 days of the date of this notice by submitting a written request and any additional records. A peer-to-peer discussion with a United medical director may be arranged on request.

Sincerely,
UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Unit
clinical_context: The patient is a 46-year-old woman with severe primary dysmenorrhea (N94.4), with pain rated 8-9/10 disabling her for two to three days each cycle. Pelvic ultrasound performed in the first quarter of 2026 was unremarkable, excluding fibroids, adenomyosis, and endometriosis as secondary causes. Over the preceding eight months she completed a trial of high-dose naproxen 500 mg twice daily, which gave inadequate relief, followed sequentially by two combined oral contraceptive formulations (a norethindrone-ethinyl estradiol pill, then a drospirenone-ethinyl estradiol pill), each carried for roughly three cycles without adequate control and the second discontinued for refractory headaches. Adult gynecology guidance identifies the levonorgestrel 52 mg intrauterine device as a first-line, guideline-concordant option for severe primary dysmenorrhea in adult women after NSAID and hormonal-pill failure. Her treating gynecologist documented that the only participating provider offering same-cycle placement had no appointment within the clinically appropriate window, and proceeded with placement to avoid another month of disabling pain, providing the basis for a retrospective gap-exception request.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: Cell matches field-for-field; denial reason squarely OON.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Frustratingly realistic with genuine exploitable gaps.

insurer_voice.reasoning: All three UHC fingerprints present; no competing tells.

denial_logic.reasoning: Authentically shoddy OON logic; real insurer entities.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Realistic dosing, drug classes, stepwise escalation.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: LNG-52 IUD recognized tx for primary dysmenorrhea.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural denial voice; neutral clinical narrative.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on cost liability, standard practice.

legal_auditor.reasoning: 180-day window, claim-file rights, no hallucinated statutes; external review omitted (intended).

scope_guard.reasoning: UHC PA, self-funded commercial, adult, member self-appeal.

date_sanity.reasoning: All dates 2025-2026, consistent timeline, determination after request.

citation_traceability.reasoning: Real United/Optum entities, realistically vague criteria, no fabricated codes.

exploitable_weaknesses: Coverage criteria rely on adolescent (ages 12-17) efficacy data misapplied to a 46-year-old adult, contradicting adult guideline-concordant first-line status of the LNG-52 IUD
Denial claims step-therapy unmet, but patient documented completed trials of high-dose naproxen and two combined oral contraceptives without relief
Network/gap-exception basis undermined by documentation that no participating provider had a same-cycle appointment in the clinically appropriate window
Three-minute determination (2:14 PM to 2:17 PM) suggests no individualized medical-necessity review
strong_defenses: Plan correctly requires in-network care or a pre-approved gap exception, and no gap exception was requested before the procedure
